Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

TCL 06, 12 + [microzodiac][via cams/gkab]

Top
t.e. 1t.e. 1

[ina a]-⸢mat⸣ d60 u an-tu₄ liš-lim

[By] the command of Anu and Antu, may it go well.

Obverse
o 1o 1

[...] ⸢GAR⸣-ma EN.NUN ig-mur u tu₁₅SI.SA₂ DU ina AN.GE₆-šu₂ dSAG.ME.GAR la₃ GUB mulUDU.IDIM.SAG.UŠ u₂-lu dṣal-bat-a-nu ina mulLU₂.ḪUN.GA₂ u₂-[lu ...]

[...] takes place and the watch finishes and the north wind blows; during this eclipse, Jupiter does not stand (and) Saturn or Mars stands in the Hireling or [...]

o 22

[u₂-lu ina] ⸢mul⸣1(IKU)gana₂ GUBiz KI.MIN ina AN.GE₆-šu₂ TUR₃ NIGIN-ma mulLUGAL ina ŠA₃-šu₂ GUBiz a-na GISKIM an-ni-ti LUGAL URIki me-ser₂ dan-nu IGI-ma : DABbat-su-ma [...]

or in] the Field; alternatively: during this eclipse, it (the Moon) is surrounded by a lunar halo and the King stands inside it; at this sign, the king of Akkad will experience severe difficulties : it will seize him and [...].

o 33

[...]⸢ki⸣ kurURIki uš-tal-pat na-mu-šu₂ BIR-MEŠ UN-MEŠ SU.GU₇ dan-nu IGI-MEŠ ŠEŠ ŠEŠ-šu₂ ru-u₈-a ru-u₈-a-šu₂ ina gišTUKUL u₂-šam-qat 2 ME MU-MEŠ ina AŠ.TE kurURIki [...]

[...], Akkad will be plundered, its pasturage scattered; the people will experience severe hunger; with a weapon a brother will defeat his brother, a friend his friend; 2 hundred years on the throne of Akkad [...];

o 44

[...] GAL₂-MEŠ DINGIR-MEŠ <ina> SU KUR BAD-MEŠ KI.MIN a-na UN-MEŠ KU₅-MEŠ UN-MEŠ BARA₂-MEŠ-ši-na TAG₄-MEŠ ARḪUŠ u SILIM-MEŠ TIL KU₅-MEŠ dEN.LIL₂ a-na KUR a-na MUNUS.ḪUL u₂-ša₂-ri kurURI⸢ki⸣ [...]

there will be [...]; the gods will withdraw (from) the interior of the land; alternatively: as for the people, they will be cut off; the people will forsake their cult daises; mercy and well-being will completely stop; Enlil will bow down towards the land for evil; Akkad [...].

o 55

dSAG!(PA)ME.GAR mulUR.GU.LA mulMUŠ

Jupiter - the Lion - the Snake.

o 66

mulAB.SIN₂

⸢mul⸣[MAŠ.MAŠ]  ; ⸢mul⸣[SIPA]

[mulAL.LUL]1

The Lion

The Furrow

The Scales

The Scorpion

Pabilsag

The Goatfish

Gula

The Field

The Hireling

The Bristle, The Bull of Heaven

The Twins, Orion

[The Crab]

o 77

E₂.AN.[NA ...]  ; [...] x [...] x  ; [...]  ; [...] x  ; [...] x

⸢E₂?⸣ [...] x  ; [...] x  ; ⸢šam⸣-nu gišESI  ; U₂.NAM.TI.LA  ; na₄san-gil-mud

SIG₄ E₂ d⸢iš-tar⸣  ; gišASAL₂  ; u₂a-<ra?>-ri-a-nu  ; na₄⸢AMAŠ⸣;MU₂.A

x SIG₄ UNUGki  ; gišdup-ra-nu  ; u₂<ši>-im-ra-nu  ; na₄NIR₂

li-tur₂  ; E₂.AN.NA  ; giššimGIR₂  ; U₂ DU₁₀.GA na₄NIR₂

E₂ dA.NUN.NA.KI  ; gišKIN₂  ; U₂.GIR₂.TAB  ; NA₄ mu-ši-tu₂

E₂ dNIN.GIŠ.ZI;DA  ; gišMES DINGIR  ; u₂MUŠ.KA.TAR  ; na₄kur-gar-ra-nu

E₂ U₄.RI₂.IN  ; gišKUR.RA  ; u₂si-ḫa  ; na₄BAL

NIBRUki E₂.KUR  ; gišEŠ₂₂  ; u₂A.ZAL.LA₂  ; na₄AN.ZAḪ

E₂ da-⸢ru₆⸣  ; gišŠE.GIŠ.I₃  ; u₂LI.⸢LI⸣  ; na₄eš₃-me-ku

[...]

[...]

Eana [...], [...] ... [...] ..., [...], [...] ..., [...] ....

... [...] ..., [...] ..., oil, ebony, life-plant, sangilmud-stone.

Brickwork of Ištar's temple, poplar, arariānu-plant, abasmû-stone.

... brickwork from Uruk, juniper, šimrānu-plant, chalcedony.

Again Eana, myrtle, a sweet plant, chalcedony.

The temple of the Anunnaku, kiškanû-tree, scorpion-plant, night-stone.

Ningišzida's temple, mēsu-tree of the god, ...-plant, kurgarrânu-stone.

The treasury temple, mountain-tree, wormwood, belemnite.

Nippur, the Ekur temple, almond-tree, azallû-plant, anzahhu-glass.

The Aru's temple, sesame-tree, ...-plant, malachite.

[...].

[...].

o 88

⸢mul⸣UR.GU.LA ḪA.LA AN.TA ša₂ dSAG.ME.GAR ina dUTU.ŠU₂.A 1.40 1.40 šu-ut da-⸢nu₃⸣ ina dUTU.ŠU₂.A 50 50 šu-ut de₂-a ina dUTU.ŠU₂.A 25 KI IRI-MEŠ an-nu-tu₂ ina mulUR.GU.LA kurURI⸢ki EŠ.BAR⸣ [...]2

The Lion, the upper portion of Jupiter, at sunset 1 40 - 1 40 those (the stars) of Anu at sunset 50 - 50 those (stars) of Ea at sunset 25. The place of these cities in the Lion [...] a decision (for) Akkad, [...].

o 99

[...] ⸢3.20⸣ A.⸢RA₂⸣ 15 ⸢50 a-na MU⸣-MEŠ ⸢LUGAL⸣ [...] 3.20 A.RA₂ 15 50 50 NIGIN₂rat d20 an-na-a NIGIN₂rat ša₂ d30 u d20 [...]

[...] 3 20 times 15 (is) 50 for the years of the king [...] ; 3 20 times 15 (is) 50; 50. This is the circumference of the Sun; the circumference of the Moon and the Sun [...].

o 1010

[KI NIG₂].⸢E₃⸣ IZI.ŠUB  ; [u] ZI KUR₂ : KI um::šu₁₄  ; [u] U₄.DA ša₂ dṣal-bat-a-nu  ; ana MUL₂ ḫi-pi₂E₃ 3.20  ; [ana] dṣal-bat-a-nu ZI KUR₂  ; dan-nu EREN₂ GAL KUR₄-ma

KI SI.SA₂ BURU₁₄ na-pa-aš₂  ; dNISABA SILIMme munusPEŠ₄-ME  ; KI SUM.SIKIL ŠE.BAR.RA  ; GANBA KI EŠ.BAR  ; LUGAL!(LU₂) ELAM.MAki  ; KI AN.TA.LU₃  ; ana ddele-bat u dGU₄.U₄!(DIŠ-)ma

KI EŠ.BAR GANBA  ; DU₃.A.BI KA la₃ GI  ; A.RA₂ la₃ SI.SA₂  ; mul₂BABBAR SIG.SIG-ma  ; dṣal-bat-a-nu KUR₄-ma  ; dSAG.UŠ KUR₄-ma

KI ri-ba ZI ⸢KUR₂⸣  ; IZI.ŠUB u imGU₂  ; dGU₄.U₄ KUR₄-ma  ; mul₂BABBAR SIG.SIG-ma  ; dṣal-bat-a-nu KUR₄-ma  ; MUNUS.KUR₂-ME GAL₂-ME

[...]  ; u GU₃ d[IŠKUR]  ; ŠEŠ-ME ana ddele-bat u dGU₄  ; ana mul₂BABBAR ki-ma KUR₄-ma  ; dṣal-bat-a-nu  ; ZI KUR₂ KUR₄-ma

[...]  ; ⸢ana ddele-bat u dGU₄.U₄⸣  ; ana dṣal-bat-a-nu  ; u dSAG.UŠ ŠEG₃  ; u ILLU KU₅ KUR ma-la-a  ; IL₂ na-zaq KUR  ; DU₃.A.BI

[...]  ; [...]  ; šeGIG u šeIMGAGA  ; ina!(TAB) KUR ZAḪ₂ ana dSAG.UŠ  ; ana ddele-bat dGU₄.U₄  ; u mul₂BABBAR SI.SA₂-ME

[...]  ; ⸢ZU₂.LUM⸣.MA ka-si-i  ; ana ddele-bat u dGU₄  ; ana dṣal-bat-a-nu  ; u dSAG.UŠ la₃ KUR₄-ME  ; mul₂BABBAR SIG.SIG-ma

[...]  ; ŠE.BAR.RA <ana> dSAG.UŠ  ; mul₂BABBAR SIG.SIG-ma  ; ana dṣal-bat-a-nu ZI KUR₂  ; KUR₄-ma ana dGU₄  ; ŠEG₃ SIG.SIG.<<SIG>>-ma

[...]  ; GU₃ dIŠKUR  ; ana dṣal-bat-a-nu  ; IZI.ŠUB ri!(AR-)ba  ; KI RA UGU GAL-ME  ; ZI TU₁₅ GAL ana dGU₄  ; : ddele-bat KUR₄-ma

⸢KI GANBA SUM.SIKIL⸣  ; ŠE.BAR.RA ri-ba  ; IZI.ŠUB ḪI.GAR KI GISKIM!(GIŠGAL)  ; u EŠ.BAR KI ri-ba  ; dan-nu SU.GU₇ dan-nu  ; GURUN DU₃.A.BI

KI ŠEG₃ [...]  ; ZI TU₁₅ LAL AL [...]  ; EŠ.BAR i₂-dig-lat  ; u!(30) pu-rat sip!(DIŠ-)parki  ; LARSAki  ; dID₂ ID₂  ; dNIN.GIR₂.SU  ; UR.SAG dNIN.LIL₂

[Sign of] victory, stroke of lightning [and] enemy attack : sign of summer [and] the light of Mars; for the constellation (gloss: break) ... king; [for] Mars: attack of a strong enemy, a great army (when) it is bright.

Sign of a thriving harvest, abundance of grain, well-being of pregnant women; sign of onions, barley, trade; sign of a decision concerning the king of Elam; sign of an eclipse; for Venus and Mercury.

Sign of a decision concerning all trade; unreliable speech, unstraight way (when) the White Star is very weak and Mars is bright and Saturn is bright.

Sign of earthquake, enemy attack, stroke of lightning and silt (when) Mercury is bright and the White Star is very weak and Mars is bright : there will be hostilities.

[...] and thunder [...] will be paired off; for Venus and Mercury, for the White Star when it is bright, and (for) Mars: enemy attack (when) it is bright.

[...] for Venus and Mercury, for Mars and Saturn: rain and flood will be cut off; the land will carry abundance; worrying of the whole land.

[...] wheat (and) emmer will be destroyed in the land; for Saturn, for Venus, Mercury and the White Star: they will thrive.

[...] dates (and) mustard; for Venus and Mercury, for Mars and Saturn (when) they are not bright (and) the White Star is very weak.

[...] barley; for Saturn (and) the White Star (when) they are very weak, for Mars: enemy attack when it is bright (and) for Mercury: rain when it is very weak.

[...] thunder; for Mars: stroke of lightning (and) earthquake; sign of ... concerning the great ones; onset of the great wind; for Mercury (and) Venus (when) they are bright.

Sign of trade of onions (and) barley, earthquake, stroke of lightning, rebellion; sign of omens and decisions; sign of a severe earthquake (and) severe famine of all fruit.

Sign of rain [...]; onset of the wind ...; decision concerning the Tigris and the Euphrates; Sippar and Larsa; the divine River, the hero Ningirsu (and) Ninlil.

o 1111

u₄-um BAD KA₂ E₂.KUR  ; [...] a-na  ; [...] KI  ; [...] KI

u₄-um BAD KA₂ d30  ; u d15 ana d30  ; u d15 na-mir-tu₂

u₄-um BAD KA₂ d30  ; d20 d15 da-nu  ; d50 de₂-a  ; u UR.SAG dNIN.GIR₂.SU

u₄-um <BAD> KA₂  ; dal-la-tu₄  ; u UR.SAG GAL dNERGALₓ(U.GUR)

u₄-um UR.SAG GALu  ; dPA.BIL.SAG  ; EN GAL dNIN.URTA

u₄-um 1(IKU)gana₂ eri-du₁₀  ; <ana> da-nu₃ u diš-tar

u₄-um UR.SAG GALu  ; dAMAR.UTU  ; u dNIN.URTA

u₄-um diš-tar  ; GAŠAN KUR.KUR dAMAR.UTU  ; LUGAL

u₄-um BAD KA₂  ; EN GALu dAMAR.UTU  ; u UR.SAG dNIN.⸢URTA⸣

u₄-um DINGIR IRI  ; UR.SAG GALu₂  ; dNIN.URTA BAD KA₂

u₄-um DINGIR IRI BAD KA₂  ; ma-a-šu₂ d30 dUTU  ; dNERGALₓ(U.GUR) EZEN MAŠ.MAŠ

u₄-um DINGIR IRI  ; dUTU DI.KU₅ KUR  ; u dŠUL.PA.E₃.A  ; BAD KA₂

The day of the opening of the gate of the Ekur temple [...] to [...] ... [...] ....

The day of the opening of Sin and Ištar's gate ; for Sin and Ištar: light.

The day of the opening of the gate of Sin, Šamaš, Ištar, Anu, Ellil, Ea and the hero Ningirsu.

The day of the opening of the gate of Allatu and the great hero Nergal.

The day of the great hero Pabilsag (and) the great lord Ninurta.

The day of the Field (and) of Eridu; for Anu and Ištar.

The day of the great hero Marduk and Ninurta.

The day of Ištar, mistress of the lands, (and) Marduk the king.

The day of the opening of the gate of the great lord Marduk and the hero Ninurta.

The day of the city god, the great hero Ninurta: opening of the gate.

The day of the city god: opening of the gate of the twins Sin (and) Šamaš, (and) Nergal: festival of the Twins.

The day of the city god, Šamaš, the judge of the land, and Šulpae : opening of the gate.

o 1212

⸢AB.ŠE.GE⸣.DA  ; ... [...] ḪA

GURUN GU₇ mul₂!?(ŠE.)1(IKU)gana₂  ; BIR.BIR di-il-ḫu

ša-ḫa-aṭ MUŠ KI.MIN  ; ša-ḫa-aṭ GIR₂.TAB

ṣal-tu₄ KU₅ DINGIR lu  ; NA DINGIR lu iš-tar MUŠ

ana SILA la₃ E₃  ; DI la₃ ŠE.GA

AB.ŠE.GE.DA de₂-a  ; u dAMAR.UTU liš-ken  ; u lik-ru-ub-⸢šu-nu⸣

ana SILA la₃ E₃  ; ni-gu-tu₂ liš-ku-un

di-nu₃ ma-gir ana dUTU  ; liš-ken ID₂ la₃ BAL  ; ana E₂ A-ME la₃ DUku  ; ṣal-tu₂ la₃ DU₃

DI la₃ ŠE.GA  ; MUŠ la₃ IGI

ṣal-tu₄ si-ip-du  ; UZU GU₄ UZU MUŠEN  ; la₃ GU₇

la₃ ŠE.GA ṣi-i-tu₂  ; ana dUTU la₃ uš-ken

ana d30 u d20!(EŠ)  ; liš-kun UZU ŠAḪ  ; la₃ GU₇

Favourable ... [...] ....

He should eat fruit; the Field, dispersal, confusion.

Attack of a snake; alternatively: attack of a scorpion.

A legal battle will be decided. God or man, god or goddess. Snake.

He should not go out to the street; a lawsuit is not favourable.

Favourable. May he prostrate himself (before) Ea and Marduk and pray to them!

He should not go out to the street. May he perform a joyful song!

A lawsuit is favourable. May he prostrate himself before Šamaš! He should not cross a river. He should not go to the water house. He should not start a legal battle.

A lawsuit is not favourable. He should not see a snake.

A legal battle, mourning. He should not eat ox meat (or) bird meat.

It is not favourable. Loss. He should not prostrate himself before Šamaš.

May he put for Sin and Šamaš! He should not eat pig meat.

o 1313

1.⸢50⸣

1.49

1.48

1.47

1.46

1.45

1.44

1.43

1.42

1.41

1.40

1 41

1 40

1 39

o 1414

EN₂ DINGIR ḫi-pi₂ ⸢eš₄-ši⸣GA TUM GA

SAG : ⸢pu⸣-tu₃

* ḪUM : ḫa-ma-<šu₂>

ina itiBAR AN SURri

TU₁₅ KALA TA 20 ana TU₁₅-2

ana sa-ma-nu I₃.NUN.NA3

Incantation: DN (gloss: new break) ....

SAG (means) : forehead.

HUM (means) : to become paralysed.

(If) Mars flashes in Nisannu (I).

(If) a strong wind from the sun to the north.

For samānu-disease: butter.

o 1515

EN₂ ḫi-pi₂ ⸢eš₄-ši⸣A GA

lu₂TUKU : ša-me

* bu-ru!(U₂.)<BURU₈> : a-ru-u₂

AN.GE₆ <ina> EN?.NUN.USAN!(UŠ.SA) ILLU

mulMAR.GID₂.DA ana AN.TA.LU₃

EN₂ ŠU.ZI.GA ŠU.ZI.GA

Incantation: (gloss: new break) ....

Creditor : heaven.

[BURU₈] (to be pronounced) BU-RU (means) : to vomit.

(If) an eclipse during the evening watch: flood.

(If) the Wagon to an eclipse.

Incantation: hand-lifting (prayer), hand-lifting (prayer).

o 1616

e-nu-ma ḫi-⸢pi₂ eš₄-ši⸣u₃ lu ḫi-pi₂

E₁₁ : a-ra-du

* SIG₂!(IG) : ši-pa!(IP-)tu₄

30 ina IGI.LA₂-šu₂ SI-MEŠ-šu₂

muldele-bat ina KASKAL šu-ut dEN.LIL₂ KURḫa

* NA li-ip-ti ina SAG.DU-šu₂

When (gloss: new break) or (gloss: break).

E₁₁ (means) : to descend.

SIG₂ (means) : wool.

(If) the horns of the moon at its appearance.

(If) Venus rises in the path of Ellil.

(If) a man's moles on his head are red (and) affected.

o 1717

⸢EN₂⸣ ḫi-pi₂ eš₄-šiIGI ḫi-pi₂

KA : pu-u₂

* KA : KA.KA ḫi-pi₂

* ina itiBAR dUDU.IDIM KURḫa

* mulMU.GID₂.KEŠ₂.DA d60 GALu ša₂ ANe

SA₅ TAG-MEŠ * NA lib₃-bu-šu₂ SI.SA₂-ME

Incantation: (gloss: new break) ... (gloss: break).

KA (means) : mouth.

KA : KA.KA (gloss: break).

(If) a planet rises in Nisannu (I).

(If) the Yoke, the great Anu of heaven.

(If) a man's bowels are in order.

o 1818

EN₂ ḫi-pi₂ eš₄-šiKIN  ; ḫi-pi₂ eš₄-šiGAR₃-MEŠ

dNANNA : gišDA BI

* IB : tu-bu-uq!(du-)tu₄

TU₁₅ ŠEG₃ ILLU U₄ NE RA ḫi-pi₂

U₂ IRI AN NA TA DIRI BAR

LUGAL NIG₂.ZI NU.EŠ₃ dEN.LIL₂.LA₂

Incantation: (gloss: new break) ... (gloss: new break) ....

Nanna : that writing board.

IB (means): corner.

(If) wind, rain (and) flood this day ... (gloss: break).

... extra and non-canonical.

Just king, nêšakku-priest of Ellil.

o 1919

x ḫi-pi₂ eš₄-šiḪI TUM₃ GU

gišAS₄ : as-lu₄

* kuKU!(LU) na-du-u

DUB ni-ṣir-tu₂ AK DINGIR ḫi-pi₂

le-ʾa-a-at an-tu₄

DIRI BAR ša₂ U₂ma GAR-šu₂

... (gloss: new break) ....

{giš}AS₄ (means) : aslu-unit.

KU (to be pronounced) KU (means) : to throw.

Tablet of secrets ... god (gloss: break).

Antu is powerful.

Extra and non-canonical of a plant's appearance.

o 2020

x BAR ḫi-pi₂ eš₄-šiU₄.DA GAL₂

GU.DI.BIR : nu-kur₂-ti

* <<ina>> irIR zu-u₂-tu₂

mul1(IKU)gana₂ MUL KUR ḫi-pi₂

an-tu₄ DABat

* NA UŠ₂-MEŠ IGI.IGIru

... (gloss: new break) there will be heat.

GU.DI.BIR (means) : enmity.

SIG₂ (means) : wool.

(If) the Field reaches the star (gloss: break).

(If) Antu afflicts.

(If) a man, blood is constantly to be seen.

o 2121

ŠU GAR ḫi-pi₂ <eš₄>-šiGAR SA.GIG  ; x GIŠGAL ḫi-pi₂ <eš₄>-šiGIŠ ZI GAN₂

ṣal-tu₄ mit-ḫu-ṣu : pu-uḫ!(U₂-)pu-uḫ!(U₂)

* ge!(ḪU-.)⸢e⸣GE₆ : mu-ši

mulGIR₂.TAB ana KI

EN₂ NA NIG₂.ŠU gišTUKUL gišMA.NU

* MUNUS UŠ₂-MEŠ maMEŠ GIŠ la₃ KU₅-MEŠ

... (gloss: new break) ... Sakikkû omen series ... (gloss: new break) ....

To fight a legal battle (means) : squabble.

IR (to be pronounced) IR (means) : sweat.

(If) the Scorpion to the zodiacal sign.

Incantation: a man, (his) goods, a weapon of ēru-wood.

(If) a woman, a lot of blood does not cease ....

o 2222

x da-nu ḫi-pi₂ <eš₄>-šiBI PA PA BI

* NA ME AD NIGIN₂ KU GAR UR GAR GIR AŠ  ; ḪU MIN

šu-pu-u₂ : pa-la-ku

* ina itiBAR dIŠKUR GU₃-šu₂ ḫi-pi₂

ina itiBAR U₄ 1-KAM ši-gu-u is-si

EN₂ SA ḪUL LU₂ BI LU₂ BI DA? [...]

... Anu (gloss: new break) ....

(If) a man ....

GE₆ (to be pronounced) GE-E (means) : night.

(If) in Nisannu (I) Adad (gloss: break) his voice.

(If) he cries out in pain on the 1st day of Nisannu (I).

Incantation: ... that man, that man ... [...].

o 2323

ḪAL ḪAL ḫi-pi₂ <eš₄>-šiAN u LU

* U₄ : im-mu U₄.U₄ : u₄-mu

mulAN.NA.ḪA.SIG.E.PAP

ANe NE-MEŠ

* NA ina GIR₃ ina ka-le-e A-ME

* NA NINDA GU₇!(NAG) A!(GAR) NAG-ma  ; ŠA₃ x RA-MEŠ ḪU? [...]

... (gloss: new break) ... and ....

U₄ (means) : daytime, U₄.U₄ (means) : days.

To break (means) : to divide off.

This heaven.

(If) a man is retaining water in (his) feet.

(If) a man eats bread and drinks water, his heart ... [...].

o 2424

* ḫi-pi₂ eš-šia-bi-šu₂  ; SAG.KI ḫi-<pi₂ eš₄>-šiu₂-lap-pat

* abAB₂ : ar-ḫu

mul₄GIR₂.TAB ana ŠA₃ ⸢EŠ?⸣ TU

mul₂LU Eki SAG!(SUR₂)DU ḫi-pi₂

DIRI BAR NIG₂.ZU LA AN KAM

* NA IGI-šu₂ LU₃.LU₃

(If) (gloss: new break) of his father, he touches the forehead (gloss: new break).

AB₂ (to be pronounced) AB (means) : cow.

The Scorpion for the interior ....

(If) the Hireling in Babylon (its) head (gloss: break).

Extra and non-canonical. Learning ....

(If) a man's eyes are troubled.

o 2525

U₄.DA ḫi-pi₂ eš!(U-)šix

* ŠAGAN : šik-kat₂ : ša₂ ŠIR₃ <za-ma>-ru

dIŠKUR ana gišḪAB

BAR ana DINGIR ḫi-pi₂

u an-tu₄ DABat

tab-nit lu₂UNUGki

Heat (gloss: new break) ....

ŠAGAN (means) : flask, concerning ŠIR₃ (means) : to sing.

Adad for the madder.

... for the god (gloss: break).

And Antu afflicts.

Offspring of the Urukean(s).

o 2626

⸢*?⸣ MUNUS ḫi-pi₂ eš!(U-)šiḪAL

* ŠU₂ : sa-ḫa-pi : ša₂ IB ḫi-pi₂TU TU

mulSAG!(PA)ME.GAR ana IGI mul₂MAŠ.TAB.BA GAL.GAL

* ⸢30⸣ ina itiBAR U₄ 1-KAM ina IGI.LA₂-šu₂

e-nu-ma ne₂-pe-šu₂ ša₂ 30 ana MAŠ₂.GE₆

⸢UZU⸣ RI : IN SU

(If) a woman (gloss: new break) ....

ŠU₂ (means) : to envelop : concerning IB (means) (gloss: break) ....

Jupiter facing the Great Twins.

(If) the Moon at its appearance on the 1st day of Nisannu (I).

When the ritual for Sin in a dream.

... (means) : ....

o 2727

[...] x ⸢AD?⸣ NA ḫi-pi₂ <eš>-⸢ši⸣NA DI

* ri-i<RI> : ra-mu-u₂

GA.RAŠ ša₂ dEN.LIL₂

⸢mul⸣UDU.IDIM ina MURU₂ ZI ḫi-pi₂

imGI₃.DA reš-tu-u₂

⸢GURUN giš⸣KIRI₆ ⸢tu-šar⸣-ra-aḫ

... (gloss: new break) ....

[RI] (to be pronounced) RI-I (means) : to throw.

Leek of Ellil.

(If) a planet in the middle ... (gloss: break).

The first oblong tablet.

You make the orchard fruit splendid.

o 2828

* DU₃ : PA UŠ GAR DU₃ LA TUM

TU₁₅ KALA TA tu₁₅KUR

* SAG!(PA)ME.GAR ana 30 DIM₄

KEŠ₂ DU₈ u₃ i-da-a-tu₂

 

....

A strong wind from the east.

(If) Jupiter approaches the moon.

Contracts, copies and written proof.

o 2929

* IRI : a-lu

31 3.30 ḫi-pi₂

IZI.GAR TA dMUATI

MUŠ GAR-šu₂

 

IRI (means) : city.

31; 3 30 (gloss: break).

An ambiguous omen from Nabu.

(If) a snake's appearance.

o 3030

* U ZI IZ MA MIN

KI-MEŠ MUL ša₂ ina ŠA₃-ši-<na?>

IZI.GAR TA dUTU.E₃ ana dUTU.ŠU₂.A

* UR? ŠU.RI.A.BI 30-AM₃

....

Regions of the stars which are inside them.

An ambiguous omen from sunrise to sunset.

... its half is 30.

o 3131

* NA AB BI LA₂ : um-mu

e-nu-ma man-za-za ša₂ DINGIR ḫi-pi₂

ina? KI? ina itiBAR U₄ 1-KAM lu₂TUR a-lid

 

... (means) : mother/heat.

When the position of the god (gloss: break).

(If) a baby is born in the zodiacal sign on the 1st day of Nisannu (I).

o 3232

ina itiAPIN ina mul₂GU.LA

ina MUL₂ ZALAG₂ GIRA₂ ḫi-pi₂

 

In Arahsamnu (VIII) in the constellation Gula.

(If) wind, rain (and) flood this day ... (gloss: break).

Reverse
r 1r 1

[... tu₁₅]⸢U₁₈⸣.LU : tu₁₅KUR.RA ⸢DU⸣ [...]

[...] the south wind (or): the east wind blows [...]

r 22

ina mul[...].AN.NA u₂-lu [...] x IGI AN.GE₆ šal-pu₂-ti₃ ⸢kur⸣[...] x [...]

in the constellation [...]... or [...] ... eclipse : ruination of the land [...] ... [...].

r 33

ŠEŠ ŠEŠ-⸢šu₂ ru-u₂-a ru-u₂-a⸣-[šu₂ ina] gišTUKUL GAZ LUGAL ⸢URI⸣ki ZI-⸢ma⸣ LUGAL ELAM.MAki ina gišTUKUL [...] x ELAM.MA⸢ki⸣ [...]

With a weapon a brother will kill his brother, a friend his friend; the king of Akkad will arise and [...] the king of Elam with a weapon; [...] ... Elam [...];

r 44

UN-MEŠ kurELAM.MAki SU.GU₇ dan-nu IGI-MEŠ a-na LUGAL ⸢URI⸣ki u UN-MEŠ-šu₂ [...]

the people of Elam will experience severe famine; for the king of Akkad and his people [...].

r 55

mul⸢UGA⸣mušen dGU₄.U₄ ⸢mulAB.SIN₂⸣

The Raven - Mercury - The Furrow.

r 66

mulAB.SIN₂

mulGIŠ.RIN₂

mulGIR₂.TAB

mulPA.BIL.SAG

mul⸢SUḪUR.MAŠ₂⸣

mulGU.LA

mul1(IKU)gana₂

mulLU₂.ḪUN.GA₂

MUL.MUL  ; mulGU₄

mulMAŠ.MAŠ  ; mulSIPA

mulAL.LUL

⸢mul⸣UR.GU.LA4

The Furrow

The Scales

The Scorpion

Pabilsag

The Goatfish

Gula

The Field

The Hireling

The Bristle, The Bull of Heaven

The Twins, Orion

The Crab

The Lion

r 77

xki U₃.SUḪ₅  ; ⸢giš⸣dul-bi u₂KUR.RA  ; na₄aš-pu₂-u₂  ; NA₄.dLAMMA

NIBRUki  ; gišASAL₂  ; u₂ḪAR.SAG  ; na₄KURnu na₄ZA.GIN₃

eri-du₁₀ gišTAŠKARIN  ; šimMAN.DU  ; na₄saḫ-ḫu-u  ; na₄DUR₂.MI.NA

ZA.MUŠ₂.ŠEŠ₂ki  ; giššu-ši gišEŠ₂₂  ; na₄ŠUBA  ; na₄GIŠ.NU₁₁.GAL

E₂ diš-tar  ; gišNU.UR₂.MA  ; u₂⸢KU?⸣ u₂LI.LI  ; na₄NI.BA  ; na₄zi-bit na₄PA

E₂ dGU.LA  ; gišNAM.TAR gišDIḪ₃  ; u₂ṣa-ṣu-un-tu₂  ; U₂.dDIM₃-ME  ; na₄pa-ru-tu₂ NA₄.GIR₂.TAB

IMki gišMA.NU  ; gišur₂-an  ; U₂ ra-pa-du  ; NA₄.MUD  ; na₄ZU₂ GE₆

IMki  ; gišMES gišŠENNUR  ; u₂kam-gu-du  ; na₄URUDU na₄A.x.PA

IMki gišESI  ; u₂kur-ka-nu-u  ; na₄AN.NA  ; NA₄ KU₃.BABBAR

DU₁₀.GARki  ; gišŠUR.MIN₃ gišLI  ; u₂a-ra-an-<tu?>  ; na₄SA.SA  ; na₄GIG.MA.NU

MU₇.MU₇ki  ; gišEREN gišBAL  ; NA₄.BAL na₄a-lal-lu₄  ; u₂KI.AN.IM  ; u₂BURU₁₄sar

E₂.NAM.ḪE₂ E₂ xki  ; gišdup-ra-nu giš⸢a?⸣-x  ; na₄sa-a-ba  ; na₄PEŠ₄.ANŠE  ; u₂KA.A.AB  ; u₂PIŠ₁₀.d⸢ID₂⸣

..., pine-tree, plane tree, nīnû-plant, jasper, lamassu-stone.

Nippur, poplar, saffron, haematite, lapis lazuli.

Eridu, box tree, sedge, sahhû-stone, breccia.

(A city), liquorice, almond tree, šubû-stone, alabaster.

Ištar's temple, pomegranate, ...-plant, ...-plant, yānibu-stone, zibītu-stone, white coral.

Gula's temple, mandragora, baltu-plant, ṣaṣumtu-plant, lamassu-plant, parūtu-alabaster, zuqiqīpu-stone.

Karkara, ēru-tree, fennel, rapādu-drugs, blood-stone, black flint.

Karkara, mēsu-tree, plum-tree, kamkadu-plant, copper, ...-stone.

Karkara, ebony, kurkanû-plant, tin, silver bead.

Damru, cypress, burāšu-juniper, arantu-grass, ...-stone, hereṣ ēri-stone.

(A city), cedar, ballukku-aromatic, touchstone, alallu-stone, qudru-seed, seed-corn.

The Enamhe temple, ..., juniper, ..., sābu-stone, biṣṣūr atāni-seashell, algae, sulphur.

r 88

x [...] ⸢mul⸣AB.SIN₂ šu-ut EN.LIL₂ ina dUTU.ŠU₂.A 1.30 1.30 šu-ut da-nu₃ ina dUTU.ŠU₂.A 45

... [...] the Furrow. Those (stars) of Ellil at sunset 1 30 - 1 30 those (stars) of Anu at sunset 45.

r 99

[...] x šu-ut de₂-a ina dUTU.ŠU₂.A 22.30 ina dUTU.E₃ 3 ina dUTU.ŠU₂.A 1.30

[...] .... Those (stars) of Ea at sunset 22;30, at sunrise 3, at sunset 1 30.

r 1010

[...] x IRI an-nu-tu ina KI mulAB.SIN₂ it-ti GISKIM kurELAM.MAki EŠ.BAR GARnu MEa GARan

[...] ... these cities in the zodiacal sign of the Furrow (together) with the sign of Elam establish a decision. You make a prediction (about it).

r 1111

[KI] ⸢SI.SA₂⸣ BURU₁₄ na-pa-aš₂  ; ⸢d⸣NISABA SILIMme munusPEŠ₄-ME  ; ⸢KI SUM⸣.SIKIL u ŠE.BAR.RA  ; ⸢GANBA⸣ KI EŠ.BAR  ; [LUGAL] ⸢ELAM.MAki⸣  ; [KI] ⸢AN.TA.LU₃⸣  ; ana ddele-bat u dGU₄.U₄!(DIŠ-)ma

KI EŠ.BAR GANBA  ; DU₃.A.BI <<u>> KA la₃ GI  ; A.RA₂ ḫi-pi₂RA  ; mul₂BABBAR SIG.SIG-ma  ; dṣal-bat-a-nu :  ; [... dSAG].⸢UŠ KUR₄-ma⸣

KI ri-ba u ZI ⸢KUR₂⸣  ; IZI.ŠUB imGU₂  ; dGU₄.U₄ KUR₄-ma  ; mul₂BABBAR SIG.SIG-ma  ; dṣal-bat-a-nu KUR₄-ma  ; ⸢MUNUS.KUR₂⸣-MEŠ GAL₂-MEŠ

KI IZI.ŠUB qi₂-mi-tu₂  ; GU₃ dIŠKUR u ZI TU₁₅  ; ŠEŠ-ME ana ddele-bat u dGU₄  ; ana mul₂BABBAR ki-ma KUR₄-ma  ; ana dṣal-bat-a-nu  ; ⸢ZI KUR₂⸣ KUR₄-⸢ma⸣

KI ŠEG₃ u ILLU  ; ana ddele-bat u dGU₄  ; ana dṣal-bat-a-nu  ; u dSAG.UŠ ŠEG₃-ME  ; u ILLU-ME KU₅-ME  ; ⸢KUR ma-la-a IL₂⸣  ; [...]

E u PA₅ la₃ SI-ME  ; dṣal-bat-a-nu KUR₄-ma  ; šeGIG šeIMGAGA  ; ina KUR ZAḪ₂  ; ana dSAG.UŠ  ; ⸢ana ddele-bat dGU₄⸣  ; [...]

KI PIŠ₁₀ SI.SA₂ BURU₁₄  ; na-pa-aš₂ dNISABA  ; SI.SA₂ gišŠE.GIŠ.I₃  ; ZU₂.LUM.MA ka-si-i  ; ana ddele-bat u dGU₄.U₄  ; ⸢ana⸣ [...]  ; ⸢la₃ KUR₄⸣ [...] ⸢SIG.SIG-ma⸣

KI GANBA SUM.SIKIL  ; ŠE.BAR.RA ana dSAG.UŠ  ; mul₂BABBAR SIG.SIG-ma  ; ana dṣal-bat-a-nu  ; ZI KUR₂ KUR₄-ma ana dGU₄  ; [...]

ana dGU₄.U₄ RAiṣ  ; GU₃ dIŠKUR  ; ana dṣal-bat-a-nu  ; be-nu IZI.ŠUB  ; u ri-ba KI RA  ; UGU GAL-ME  ; [...]

KI GANBA SUM.SIKIL  ; ŠE.BAR.RA ri-ba  ; IZI.ŠUB ḪI.GAR  ; KI GISKIM!(GIŠGAL) EŠ!(MAN)BAR!(NUN-)ME  ; u KI ri-<ba> dan-nu  ; SU.GU₇ <<u>> dan-nu  ; [...]

KI ŠEG₃ u ILLU  ; ZI TU₁₅ LAL AL BU IM  ; EŠ.BAR i₂-dig-lat  ; u pu-rat sip-parki  ; u LARSAki  ; dID₂ ID₂  ; [...]

KI NIG₂.E₃ IZI.⸢ŠUB⸣  ; ZI KUR₂ KI um-⸢šu₁₄⸣  ; u U₄.DA dṣal-bat-a-[nu]  ; ana mul₂BABBAR NIG₂.E₃ ⸢3⸣.[20]  ; ana dṣal-bat-a-nu  ; ZI KUR₂ dan-⸢nu  ; EREN₂ GAL⸣ KUR₄-⸢ma⸣

[Sign] of a thriving harvest, abundance of grain, well-being of pregnant women; sign of onions and barley, trade; sign of a decision concerning [the king] of Elam; [sign] of an eclipse; for Venus and Mercury.

Sign of a decision concerning all of trade; unreliable speech, the way (gloss: break) ... (when) the White Star is very weak and Mars [...] Saturn is bright.

Sign of earthquake and enemy attack, stroke of lightning, silt (when) Mercury is bright and the White Star is very weak and Mars is bright : there will be hostilities.

Sign of a stroke of lightning, burning; thunder and the onset of the wind will be paired off; for Venus and Mercury, for the White Star when it is bright, for Mars: enemy attack (when) it is bright.

Sign of rain and flood; for Venus and Mercury, for Mars and Saturn: rains and floods will be cut off; the land will carry abundance [...].

Dykes and ditches will not be in order (when) Mars is bright; wheat (and) emmer will be destroyed in the land; for Saturn, for Venus, Mercury [...].

Sign of the riverbank, a thriving harvest, abundance of grain, thriving of sesame, dates (and) mustard; for Venus and Mercury, for [...] (when) they are not bright (and) [...] is very weak.

Sign of the trade of onions (and) barley; for Saturn (and) the White Star (when) they are very weak, for Mars: enemy attack when it is bright (and) for Mercury [...].

For Mercury: he will devastate, thunder; for Mars: epilepsy, stroke of lightning and earthquake; sign of ... concerning the great ones [...].

Sign of the trade of onions (and) barley, earthquake, stroke of lightning, rebellion; sign of omens (and) decisions, and sign of a severe earthquake (and) severe famine [...].

Sign of rain and flood; onset of the wind ...; decision concerning the Tigris and the Euphrates; Sippar and Larsa; the divine River [...].

Sign of victory, stroke of lightning (and) enemy attack : sign of summer and the light of Mars; for the White Star: victory of the king; for Mars: attack of a strong enemy, a great army (when) it is bright.

r 1212

u₄-um BAD KA₂ d30  ; u d15 ana d30  ; u d15 na-mir-tu₂

u₄-um BAD KA₂ d30  ; d20 d15 da-nu  ; d50 de₂-a  ; u UR.SAG dNIN.GIR₂.SU

u₄-um BAD KA₂  ; dal-lat  ; u UR.SAG GALu  ; dNERGALₓ(U.GUR)

u₄-⸢um⸣ [UR.SAG GALu]  ; dPA.BIL.SAG  ; EN GALu dNIN.URTA

u₄-⸢um⸣ 1(IKU)gana₂  ; eri-du₁₀ ana da-nu  ; u diš-tar

u₄-um UR.SAG GALu  ; dAMAR.UTU u dNIN.URTA

u₄-um diš-tar  ; GAŠAN KUR.KUR  ; u dAMAR.UTU LUGAL

u₄-um BAD KA₂  ; EN GALu dAMAR.UTU  ; u UR.SAG dNIN.URTA

[u₄-um DINGIR IRI]  ; [UR.SAG GALu₂]  ; ⸢d⸣[NIN.URTA]; ⸢BAD KA₂⸣

[...]

[...]

[...]

The day of the opening of Sin and Ištar's gate ; for Sin and Ištar: light.

The day of the opening of the gate of Sin, Šamaš, Ištar, Anu, Ellil, Ea and the hero Ningirsu.

The day of the opening of the gate of Allatu and the great hero Nergal.

The day [of the great hero] Pabilsag (and) the great lord Ninurta.

The day of the Field (and) of Eridu; for Anu and Ištar.

The day of the great hero Marduk and Ninurta.

The day of Ištar, mistress of the lands, and Marduk the king.

The day of the opening of the gate of the great lord Marduk and the hero Ninurta.

[The day of the city god, the great hero Ninurta]: opening of the gate.

[...].

[...].

[...].

r 1313

ka-liš ŠE.GA d30  ; u d⸢UTU⸣ liš-ken ana MUNUS-šu₂  ; ⸢TE⸣[ḫe] ŠA₃.ḪUL₂

di-nu₃ ḫi-pi₂ ; ana dUTU ḫi-pi₂ ; UŠ TAK ḫi-pi₂

ana IGI-ka ṣal-tu₄  ; MUŠ GIR₂.TAB  ; u IGI la₃ ⸢SIG₅⸣

⸢ana di⸣-nu₃ la₃ E₃  ; ⸢ṣal⸣-tu₄ iṣ-ṣa-al

ana de₂-a liš-ken  ; ana de₂-a u dIŠKUR  ; KURUM₆-su liš-ku-un

ka-liš ŠE.GA  ; ni-gu-tu₂  ; liš-ku-un

ŠA₃.ḪUL₂ ana MUNUS-šu₂  ; TEḫe GURUN GU₇  ; be-ra-tu₂ ZUKUM

di-nu ma-gir  ; GA la₃ NAG  ; uš-ši d30  ; u d20 GARun

ṣal-tu₄ x [...]  ; A-MEŠ x [...]  ; la₃ ŠU x [...]

[...]

[...]

[...]

Completely favourable. May he prostrate himself (before) Sin and Šamaš! He should approach his wife; joy.

Lawsuit (gloss: break) to Šamaš (gloss: break) ... (gloss: break).

For you: legal battle, snake, scorpion and a bad eye.

He will not go out to a lawsuit; he will continually fight a legal battle.

May he prostrate himself before Ea! May he provide Ea and Adad with rations!

Completely favourable. May he perform a joyful song!

Joy! He should approach his wife, eat fruit, (and) trample the dividing baulks!

A lawsuit is favourable. He should not drink milk. May he put the foundations of (a temple for) Sin and Šamaš!

Legal battle ... [...], water ... [...] not ... [...].

[...].

[...].

[...].

r 1414

1.30

1.29

1.28

1.27

1.26

1.25

1.24

1.23

1.22

[1.21]

[1.20]

[1.19]

1 30

1 29

1 28

1 27

1 26

1 25

1 24

1 23

1 22

[1 21]

[1 20]

[1 19]

r 1515

TA UGU gišDA SUMUNbar GABA.RI UNUGki SAR-ma IGI.TAB

Written and checked from an old writing board, a copy from Uruk.

r 1616

⸢ṭup⸣-pi md60—EN-šu₂-nu ⸢lu₂⸣GALA d60 ma-ru₃ ša₂ mNIG₂.SUM.MU—d60 ma-ru₃ md30—TI—ER₂ TIR.AN.NAki u₂ qat₃ md60—AD—GUR A-šu₂ lu₂UMBISAG * U₄ d60 ⸢dEN⸣.[LIL₂ ...]

Tablet of Anu-belšunu, lamentation priest of Anu, son of Nidintu-Anu, son of Sin-leqi-unninni, the Tiranaean. Hand of Anu-ab-uter, his son, scribe of (the Series) 'When Anu, Ellil'

r 1717

[... m]⸢an⸣-ti-ʾi-ku-su ⸢LUGAL⸣ MUD d60 dEN.LIL₂ u dIDIM ina šur-qa la₃ TUM₃-šu₂

[...] Antiochus, king. He who reveres Anu, Ellil and Ea shall not take it away by means of theft.

1the orientation of the 12 names of the zodiacal signs is turned 90 degrees to the right.

2there is a KI-sign above the KUR of {kur}URI{ki#}.

3From this line onwards, the text reads down the columns, not across the rows

4the orientation of the 12 names of the zodiacal signs is turned 90 degrees to the right.

Created by Greta Van Buylaere for the AHRC-funded GKAB Project, 2010 and released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/cams/gkab/P363685
